Simple queries with %dsl or %%dsl 2. Loop queries with %dslloop or %%dslloop 3. Returning dataframes: %dsldf and %%dsldf 4. Looped dataframe queries: %dslloopdf and %%dslloopdf 5. response.content: Researcher IDs and Profiles - Research Profiles and Assessment - LibGuides at Chapman University Skip to Main Content Chapman University LibGuides Research Profiles and Assessment Researcher IDs and Profiles Search this Guide Search Research Profiles and Assessment: Researcher IDs and Profiles Information about metrics, citations, impact factors, altmetrics, and researcher profiles and IDs. Home Citation Analysis and Impact Factors Toggle Dropdown Finding Articles That Cite Another Altmetrics Researcher IDs and Profiles Scholarly Communications Librarian You can get help for research in several ways. Contact the Scholarly Communications librarian, Kristin Laughtin-Dunker, at laughtin@chapman.edu or (714) 532-7769 Call the Reference Desk at (714) 532-7714 Chat with us at LibChat during Reference Desk hours Email us at Quick Reference Ask the KnowledgeBase About Researcher IDs Researcher IDs are useful for creating an authoritative identity as a researcher and scholar. They can prevent you from being confused with other academics with similar names or consolidate papers you may have written under different monikers by linking all of your work to a single profile. Researcher IDs and Profiles (8:25) This link opens in a new window Learn how to create researcher profiles in Google Scholar and ORCID. Please note: Web of Science/Publons have recently updated their researcher ID process, and this part of the video is no longer accurate. ORCID ORCID (Open Researcher and Contributor ID) is an open, non-profit, community-driven effort to create and maintain a registry of unique researcher identifiers and a transparent method of linking research activities and outputs to these identifiers. ORCID provides two core functions: (1) a registry to obtain a unique identifier and manage a record of activities, and (2) APIs that support system-to-system communication and authentication. Many publishers have begun requiring authors to use an ORCID identifier. ORCID This link opens in a new window ORCID registration This link opens in a new window Additional links: Importing works from Google Scholar into ORCID This link opens in a new window ORCID Affiliation Manager For Chapman researchers, the Leatherby Libraries can add verified affiliations with Chapman University to your ORCID profile. To get started, send your department, start date, and title to laughtin@chapman.edu . We will add it to the ORCID Affiliation Manager and generate a link for you to authorize and confirm that Chapman University can add this information to your record. After granting permission, an employment entry will be added to your ORCID record. The source of this entry in ORCID will be Chapman University. Note: it may take up to 5 minutes for your ORCID record to be updated. Frequently Asked Questions Why should I allow Chapman University to update my ORCID record? An affiliation with Chapman University as the source signals to other systems that the information comes from an authoritative source and that they have vouched for the fact that you are indeed affiliated with them. As ORCIDs are increasingly used to autofill manuscript submission or grant application forms, this allows your data to be reused with a high degree of confidence. What else should I know? Although Chapman University will only add affiliation information to your ORCID record at this time, the permission you grant us now would allow us to add other information to your ORCID record (such as awards) in the future. We will notify you if we plan to do this. Researcher control is central to everything ORCID does. If you no longer want Chapman University to edit your ORCID record, you can revoke permission at any time. See Revoking Permission for instructions. Affiliation information added to your ORCID record is subject to ORCID s Privacy Policy . Per this policy, you can control the visibility of this information in ORCID . The source data, however, is controlled by Chapman University and is subject to our
